title: cerastium pentandrum subsp. fallax
intro: Cerastium pentandrum subsp. fallax, also known as the false mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cerastium pentandrum subsp. fallax include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing, making it a popular choice for topical applications. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a versatile remedy for various health issues.
